intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels less clinical and more intimate when using dark backgrounds with gentle depth cues rather than bright, flat surfaces
facade.label: Dark cards with soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: User favors moody, layered aesthetics over sterile brightness—lean into dark mode, subtle gradients, and dimensional card treatments
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and airiness—move toward light backgrounds, minimal shadows, and flat modern design

evidence.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels less clinical and more intimate when using dark backgrounds with gentle depth cues rather than bright, flat surfaces
evidence.implication: User favors moody, layered aesthetics over sterile brightness—lean into dark mode, subtle gradients, and dimensional card treatments

facade.hypothesis: A conversational, friendly tone in microcopy and notifications will feel more supportive than clinical language for long-term health tracking
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, human-centered voice that encourages habit formation through approachability rather than authority
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers data-forward, clinical, or neutral tone that positions the dashboard as objective measurement tool rather than motivational companion

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer pre-summarized weekly snapshots over raw daily data points in a health dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Aggregation level — granular daily metrics vs rolled-up periodic summaries
facade.label: Weekly summary cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should prominently feature weekly or monthly digest cards; users want narrative momentum over exhaustive detail
facade.rejectImplies: Users need to see all data points day-by-day; they'll do their own pattern-spotting; summaries obscure the truth they're tracking

facade.hypothesis: Users want a persistent left navigation with flexible card-based content areas, enabling both quick experiment access and detailed metric views in one screen
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Probe dense sidebar patterns, multi-column card layouts, and persistent context switching
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab-based or modal navigation; test list-view alternatives; investigate full-width immersive layouts

facade.hypothesis: Users building a health dashboard benefit from fluid animations between state changes rather than instant updates, as they help track progress narratives over time
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize easing functions and interpolated transitions for metric updates, chart redraws, and view switches; users value the journey between data points
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer snappy, instant feedback with minimal animation; motion is overhead; design for zero-latency interactions and static layouts

facade.hypothesis: Users building personal health dashboards prefer organic, approachable color palettes (earth tones, warm neutrals) over clinical or sterile color schemes, signaling safety and long-term commitment rather than medical coldness.
facade.label: Warm, lived-in colors
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into natural, muted, warm palette — terracotta, sage, warm gray, cream. Avoid bright primaries and high contrast.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore cooler, cleaner, more minimal or tech-forward palettes — cool grays, crisp whites, accent colors. Signal precision over coziness.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory feedback on progress vs. quiet, non-judgmental tracking space
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative reveal of data over time

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative reveal of data over time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard feels more approachable and less intimidating when buttons, cards, and containers use generous border radius rather than sharp 90-degree angles
facade.label: Rounded corners everywhere
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace rounded UI elements throughout the interface—soften form factors to create a welcoming, less clinical feeling that complements the dark mode mood already established
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer geometric precision and defined boundaries—explore crisp edges, structured grids, and angular forms that provide visual clarity and a more technical/scientific aesthetic
